title: Weighted Shortest Paths and Relaxation · Correctness Defense
summary: Choose safe routes across positive, negative, and unreachable weighted networks. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a multi-algorithm route engine with path proofs. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State and apply edge relaxation while preserving predecessor evidence.
Run Dijkstra only under compatible nonnegative weights and stale-entry handling.
Use repeated relaxation to find shortest paths and reachable negative-cycle evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Relaxation correctness-and-cost proof for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Relaxation assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Route edges A→B:3, A→C:6, B→C:-1, C→D:3. Select an algorithm from weight preconditions, return a concrete path, and report any reachable negative-cycle witness.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Relaxation has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Relaxation precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.
